global.description:
Values in the .global section can be accessed by both parent and child subcharts as
.Values.global.*: https://helm.sh/docs/chart_template_guide/subcharts_and_globals/

agentBackendDomain.description: Domain where the Agent Backend service listens. Evaluated as a template
agentBackendDomain.title: agentBackendDomain

httpRoute.description:
Gateway API (HTTPRoute) defaults shared across the parent chart and all subcharts. Each
subchart's local `httpRoute.*` value takes precedence when set; otherwise the global is used.
HTTPRoutes attach to a Gateway you already manage (via `parentRefs`); this chart does not
create the Gateway or GatewayClass. TLS is configured on the Gateway listener, not here.

annotations.description:
Annotations merged into the HTTPRoute. Local `httpRoute.annotations` wins on key
collision. Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

enabled.description:
Enable HTTPRoute for this chart. OR'd with the chart's local `httpRoute.enabled` so
setting this once at the parent enables all subchart HTTPRoutes. Independent from
`ingress.enabled`
enabled.title: enabled

extraLabels.description:
Extra labels merged into the HTTPRoute. Local `httpRoute.extraLabels` wins on key
collision. Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

matchType.description:
Default path match type applied when `httpRoute.matchType` is not set. One of
`PathPrefix`, `Exact`, or `RegularExpression`
matchType.title: matchType

parentRefs.description:
Parent Gateway(s) this chart's HTTPRoute attaches to. Applied when the local
`httpRoute.parentRefs` is not set. Each entry accepts `name` (required), `namespace`,
`sectionName`, `port`, `kind`, and `group`. Evaluated as a template
parentRefs.title: parentRefs

path.description: Default path applied to HTTPRoute rules when `httpRoute.path` is not set
path.title: path

httpRoute.title: httpRoute

imageCredentials.description:
Optional credentials to log in and fetch images from a private registry. These credentials
are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentials.title: imageCredentials

imageCredentialsSecrets.description:
Optional list of existing Secrets containing image pull credentials to use for pulling
images from private registries. These Secrets are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentialsSecrets.title: imageCredentialsSecrets

ingress.description:
Ingress defaults shared across the parent chart and all subcharts. Each subchart's local
`ingress.*` value takes precedence when set; otherwise the global is used.

annotations.description:
Annotations merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.annotations` wins on key collision.
Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

defaultPathType.description:
Default path type applied to ingress rules when `ingress.defaultPathType` is not set.
`Prefix` works for nginx, traefik, AWS ALB, and most modern controllers.
defaultPathType.title: defaultPathType

enabled.description:
Enable Ingress for this chart. OR'd with the chart's local `ingress.enabled` so
setting this once at the parent enables all subchart Ingresses.
enabled.title: enabled

extraLabels.description:
Extra labels merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.extraLabels` wins on key collision.
Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

ingressClassName.description: Default ingress class name applied when `ingress.ingressClassName` is not set
ingressClassName.title: ingressClassName

path.description:
Default path applied to ingress rules when `ingress.path` is not set.
AWS ALB users should override to `/*`.
path.title: path

tls.description: TLS entries concatenated with the local `ingress.tls`. Evaluated as a template
tls.title: tls

ingress.title: ingress

mcpDomain.description: Domain where Seqera MCP listens. Evaluated as a template
mcpDomain.title: mcpDomain

platformServiceAddress.description:
Seqera Platform Service name: can be the internal Kubernetes hostname or an external ingress
hostname. Evaluated as a template.
Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent
`platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServiceAddress.title: platformServiceAddress

platformServicePort.description:
(int) Seqera Platform Service port.
Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent
`platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServicePort.title: platformServicePort

global.title: global

httpRoute.description:
Gateway API routing. Renders HTTPRoute objects that attach to an existing Gateway (managed
outside this chart) via `parentRefs`. Independent from `ingress` — you may enable either.
TLS is terminated on the Gateway listener, so there is no TLS block here.

annotations.description: HTTPRoute annotations. Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

extraHosts.description:
Additional hosts you want to route. Each host becomes its own HTTPRoute. Evaluated as a
template
extraHosts.title: extraHosts

extraLabels.description: Additional labels for the HTTPRoute object. Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

matchType.description:
Path match type for the HTTPRoute. One of `PathPrefix`, `Exact`, or `RegularExpression`.
When empty, falls back to `global.httpRoute.matchType`
matchType.title: matchType

parentRefs.description:
Gateway(s) this HTTPRoute attaches to. When empty, falls back to
`global.httpRoute.parentRefs`. Each entry accepts `name` (required), `namespace`,
`sectionName`, `port`, `kind`, and `group`. Evaluated as a template
parentRefs.title: parentRefs

path.description: Path for the main HTTPRoute rule. When empty, falls back to `global.httpRoute.path`
path.title: path

httpRoute.title: httpRoute

digest.description: Container image digest in the format `sha256:1234abcdef`
digest.title: digest

pullSecrets.title: pullSecrets

enabled.description: Enable init containers that coordinate startup dependencies
enabled.title: enabled

enabled.description: Enable wait for MySQL init container before starting the main container
enabled.title: enabled

extraEnvVars.description:
Additional environment variables for the init container.
The special variable `MYSQL_EXTRA_ARGS` is appended verbatim to the `mysql` readiness
check command, and can be used to pass TLS flags when connecting to managed MySQL services
such as AWS RDS or Azure Database for MySQL that require certificate verification.
Pair with `extraVolumeMounts` to mount the CA certificate into the container.
Example (TLS with CA certificate mounted from a volume):
extraEnvVars:
  - name: MYSQL_EXTRA_ARGS
    value: "--ssl-ca=/certs/ca.pem --ssl-mode=VERIFY_IDENTITY"
extraEnvVars.title: extraEnvVars

extraVolumeMounts.description:
Additional volume mounts for the init container. Use this to mount CA certificates
(e.g. from a Secret or ConfigMap populated by an extraInitContainer) so that
`MYSQL_EXTRA_ARGS` can reference them.
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

nodePort.description:
Service node port, only used when service.type is Nodeport or LoadBalancer
Choose port between 30000-32767, unless the cluster was configured differently than default
nodePort.title: nodePort

targetPort.description:
Port on the pod/container that the Service forwards traffic to (can be a number or
named port, distinct from the Service's external port)
targetPort.title: targetPort

type.description:
Service type.
Note: ingresses using AWS ALB require the service to be NodePort

create.description:
Whether to create a ServiceAccount. When `true`, the ServiceAccount named by
`serviceAccount.name` is created (a `<release>-<chart>-sa` name is generated when it is unset).
Set to `false` to use an existing, externally-managed ServiceAccount referenced by
`serviceAccount.name`.
create.title: create

imagePullSecretNames.description: Names of Secrets containing credentials to pull images from registries
imagePullSecretNames.title: imagePullSecretNames

name.description:
Name of the ServiceAccount used by this chart's workloads. When empty, a name is generated
from the release name. Applies whether the ServiceAccount is created by this chart
(`create: true`) or managed externally (`create: false`).
name.title: name

startupProbe.description: Configure extra options for the startup probe

Consecutive failures during startup before killing the container (instead of immediate
restarts)
failureThreshold.title: failureThreshold

path.description: HTTP GET path for startup probe
path.title: path

port.description: HTTP GET port for startup probe. Evaluated as a template
port.title: port

initialDelaySeconds.title: initialDelaySeconds

periodSeconds.description: Often set longer to avoid frequent checks while starting
periodSeconds.title: periodSeconds

successThreshold.description:
Number of consecutive successes required to consider startup complete and enable
liveness/readiness
successThreshold.title: successThreshold

timeoutSeconds.description: Can be longer to allow slow initialization responses
timeoutSeconds.title: timeoutSeconds

startupProbe.title: startupProbe

tokenEncryptionKey.description:
Token encryption key (must be a valid Fernet key). Define the value as a String or a Secret,
not both at the same time. If not defined, a random Fernet key will be auto-generated at each
deployment.
WARNING: Always explicitly set this value or use an existing secret when using Kustomize.
Auto-generated random values are incompatible with Kustomize. When upgrading releases via
Kustomize, Helm cannot query the cluster to check if a secret already exists, causing it to
regenerate a new random value on each upgrade
tokenEncryptionKey.title: tokenEncryptionKey

tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretKey.description: Key in the existing Secret containing the token encryption key
tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretKey.title: tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretKey

tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretName.description:
Name of an existing Secret containing the token encryption key.
Note: the Secret must already exist in the same namespace at the time of deployment
tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretName.title: tokenEncryptionKeyExistingSecretName
